intro: Aria brevipetiolata, also known as Aria, is a shrub native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used to aid in the relief of cold and flu symptoms, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Aria brevipetiolata include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and triterpenes, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic effects. Aria brevipetiolata is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to support immune function and alleviate respiratory issues.
